title: REST or RPC/gRPC?
intro: Sort each trait or scenario by the API style it points to.

cards.text: Resource-centric: nouns in the path, verbs as HTTP methods
Public API consumed by many unknown third-party clients
Responses are cacheable and human-readable JSON
HATEOAS links let clients discover related actions
Action-centric: the interface is a list of callable functions
Low-latency internal call between two of your own microservices
Binary protobuf over HTTP/2 with a strongly-typed contract
Bidirectional streaming of audio frames between services

debrief: REST is resource/noun-centric, stateless, cacheable and loosely coupled — ideal for public, evolvable web APIs consumed by clients you don't control. RPC/gRPC is action-centric with tight, strongly-typed coupling and high performance (binary protobuf, HTTP/2, streaming) — ideal for internal service-to-service calls where both ends ship together. The rule of thumb: REST at the edge, gRPC between your own services.
